nonBlockingIssues.description: prisma CLI is in dependencies rather than devDependencies. Works but non-standard.

Podcaster-reuse test is misleading: uses placeholder channelUrl pattern so each video gets its own Podcaster. Test name suggests reuse but doesn't verify VAL-INGEST-005 (which requires real channel data).
No explicit 500 error handling in POST /api/episodes. Unexpected DB errors result in generic Next.js 500.
No type validation for timestamp field — non-numeric values pass validation and hit Prisma constraint, resulting in 500 instead of descriptive 400.
POST /api/chat/end doesn't verify conversationId existence — nonexistent IDs treated as zero-message no-op (200) instead of 404.
SSE error event format ('data: [ERROR]') is unstructured — clients can't distinguish error type.
All buildPodcasterProfile errors mapped to 503, conflating DB errors with LLM unreachability.
Timer-based assertion (setTimeout 50ms) for fire-and-forget profile build test could be flaky in CI.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed stale DATABASE_URL default in environment.md: changed 'file:./dev.db' to 'file:./prisma/dev.db' to match actual code.
Removed stale dotenv note from environment.md: 'dotenv is imported in prisma.config.ts but NOT in package.json' — the import was removed in foundation-housekeeping.

Created .factory/library/testing-patterns.md documenting: per-test-file SQLite database convention (to avoid lock conflicts), test file import pattern (relative paths), and deterministic profile ID convention.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add testing convention: 'Each test file must use its own SQLite database file to avoid lock conflicts. Name pattern: prisma/test-<feature>.db. Add new test DB files to .gitignore.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features api-episodes-crud, api-chat-endpoint, and api-profiles-build all independently discovered and solved SQLite lock conflicts by creating separate test databases. This pattern is now documented in .factory/library/testing-patterns.md but should also be in AGENTS.md as a normative convention.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Workers should document knowledge discoveries in the feature that discovered them, not in unrelated feature commits. Use a separate commit if the documentation spans multiple features.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: api-chat-endpoint worker added youtube-transcript library limitations to environment.md in their commit (aefbf40), but this knowledge was discovered during episodes feature work. Cross-feature documentation in unrelated commits causes git blame confusion.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Backend-worker skill's 'Write Tests First' step could note that infrastructure/setup features are exempt from strict TDD — tests can only be written after the infrastructure being set up (vitest, Prisma, etc.).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-housekeeping worker noted this in skillFeedback: tests couldn't be written before vitest was installed and Prisma was configured.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker suggested backend-worker skill should mention npx shim workarounds.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — AGENTS.md already has a 'Known Issues' section documenting broken npx shims and direct path workarounds. Adding to the skill would be redundant.

rejectedObservations.observation: The Prisma SQLite adapter pattern is well-documented in AGENTS.md.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — This is a positive observation, not an action item. AGENTS.md already covers this adequately.

rejectedObservations.observation: Placeholder channelUrl pattern should be documented in architecture docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — The code contains comments explaining the placeholder pattern, and the limitation is inherent to the youtube-transcript library which is already documented in environment.md. Adding to AGENTS.md would be premature since this will be resolved when channel data is available.
